error_message: Deploy preflight takes 5-6 min per invocation; PHP lint walks vendor/
root_cause: find at line 206 walked all .php under THEME_DIR with no prunes. 3,881 files total but only 124 are the theme's; the other 3,756 are Composer vendor deps. At 0.36s per php -l, vendor-lint alone cost 5-6 min per deploy. vendor/ was already gitignored, excluded from tarball, and excluded from phpcs — the lint walk was the last path that didn't get the memo.
fix: Added -prune for vendor, node_modules, tests, .git. Replaced hardcoded /opt/homebrew/bin/php with `command -v php` fallback. Added file count to success log. Scope: 124 (was 3,881). Expected preflight: ~45s (was ~5-6 min).

error_message: No concurrency guard; two deploys can race on the remote .old.$swap_id rename
root_cause: Hot-swap uses a timestamp+PID swap_id for rename-aside-and-rename-into-place. Two parallel deploys produce different swap_ids but race on the same live path. If deploy B's rename lands between A's rename-aside and rename-into-place, A loses its backup anchor and B's backup points at A's half-extracted tarball.
fix: Added acquire_deploy_lock() using /tmp/skyyrose-deploy.lock with PID stored inside. Stale lock (PID dead) reclaimed; live lock refuses second deploy with exit 2. release_deploy_lock fires in cleanup trap.

error_message: If verify_live() fails, site stays broken; no auto-rollback
root_cause: verify_live returned non-zero but main() only logged and exited 1 — the broken theme remained live. Old theme was already deleted by the immediate rm -rf .old.$swap_id at end of try_rsync, so no backup to restore.
fix: Added auto_rollback() that SSHs remote, reverses the swap (mv broken to .failed.$ts, restore .old.$ts into live path), and flushes caches. Changed try_rsync to NOT delete .old.$ts immediately. Cleanup trap inspects ROLLBACK_REQUESTED and calls auto_rollback on verify failure. Exit code 3 signals rollback occurred.

error_message: .old.$swap_id deleted immediately after swap; no rollback anchor
root_cause: Original compound command chained `mv new -> live && rm -rf .old.$swap_id`. Bounded disk but destroyed the only artifact auto-rollback could restore from.
fix: Removed the immediate rm. Added deterministic retention: after every swap, ls .old.* on remote and xargs rm -rf all but the 2 most recent. Bounded disk, rollback anchor preserved.

error_message: verify_live checked only homepage; CSS/JS breakage silent when WP-CDN cached HTML
root_cause: verify_live() only curled the homepage. If WP-CDN served cached HTML but new CSS/JS 404'd from origin, check passed while site was visibly broken. Also: no per-phase timing, no structured log.
fix: Extended verify_live() to curl style.css, immersive.css, immersive.js with cache-busting. Every asset URL must return 200. Added DEPLOY_CHECK_ASSETS env var for project-specific additions. Added phase_start/phase_end/phase_summary (bash 3.2 compatible scalars — no associative arrays). Tee'd stdout+stderr to /tmp/skyyrose-deploy-<ts>.log.

error_message: tar -czf wastes CPU compressing already-compressed media (WebP/GLB/JPG/PNG)
root_cause: Theme payload ~380 MB, of which ~316 MB is already-compressed binary media. gzip achieves ~0% reduction on those formats but costs single-threaded CPU per deploy.
fix: Runtime-detect tar --zstd and zstd binary. When available, use `tar --zstd -cf` (5x faster, similar ratio on text parts). Fallback to plain `-cf` (no compression) when zstd absent — still faster than gzip on pre-compressed media. Matching remote extract flag. Tarball extension adjusts.

error_message: SSHPASS exported for entire deploy duration, leaking credential to every child process
root_cause: build_ssh_cmd() unconditionally exported SSHPASS as soon as the script started; remained in env for preflight, tar, every subprocess until exit. On shared systems, `ps e <pid>` or /proc/<pid>/environ leak the password.
fix: Moved SSHPASS export from build_ssh_cmd() into try_rsync() just before scp. Added `unset SSHPASS` immediately after remote extract. Exposure window shrunk from entire deploy (~7 min) to sub-second. No functional change — sshpass -e reads from env at invocation.

error_message: 43.4 MB orphan GIF bloating every deploy payload for no reason
root_cause: GIF (1920x1080, 326 frames) superseded by rose-gold-spin.mp4 (3.86 MB) and rose-gold-spin.webm (2.34 MB) on 2026-04-14 but source GIF never removed. Zero references anywhere in theme PHP/CSS/JS/JSON. Every deploy paid the tar + upload cost for a file that would never be served.
fix: Pre-delete grep confirmed zero references. Deleted the .gif. MP4/WebM remain as modern animated-logo assets. Savings: 43.4 MB off every future tarball (11% payload reduction).

error_message: template-preorder-gateway.php: (a) missing kids-capsule collection, (b) $fallback_products uses nonexistent SKUs (br-hoodie, lh-hoodie, sg-hoodie etc.), (c) wc_get_products() query with fallback to fake data, (d) card args missing image_url and correct permalink

error_message: TypeError: e.target.closest is not a function — SVG elements and other non-Element nodes dispatched to document-level event listeners don't have .closest()
root_cause: SVG child elements (path, circle, use, etc.) are SVGElement not HTMLElement and may not implement .closest() in older browsers. document-level capture listeners receive events from any element on the page including SVG internals.
fix: Added typeof guard before every .closest() call: getCardData(), mouseenter listener, mouseleave listener, click listener — each now checks !el || typeof el.closest !== 'function' before calling.

fix: Replaced both references (path and URL) to skyyrose-mascot-reference.png with skyy-canonical.jpeg in the mascot fallback block of 404.php.

error_message: Tripo generate_multiview_image hallucinated brand canon on 30 SKUs (120 renders) — invented 'rose-on-cloud' motif on br-001, rendered br-011 hockey jersey as cyan/teal hoodie with invented crest, applied off-canon patch to wrong location on sg-007 signature beanie
root_cause: Tripo `generate_multiview_image` template runs FLUX.1 Kontext with naked image-only input — accepts NO prompt, NO logo overlay, NO dossier branding spec, NO palette token. With no canon anchor, FLUX defaults to training-set priors. Dispatch boundary had no guard distinguishing branded SKUs (which Tripo cannot render correctly) from unbranded clean tech-flats (which it can). Additionally, SKUs with empty catalog `image` column silently fell back to `front_model_image` (a model-on shot), which the template re-builds from scratch — changing garment type entirely (jersey → hoodie). No QA gate caught the regression; STOP-AND-SHOW manifest showed cost only, no output preview.
fix: scripts/tripo_dispatch.py — added classify_skus() function that blocks at the dispatch boundary: (1) SKUs whose dossier frontmatter has `logo_reference:` populated are blocked as BRANDED with directive to route via ADK render_pipeline. (2) SKUs with empty `image` column or missing tech-flat file are blocked as NO TECH-FLAT. --force-branded escape hatch added with WARNING and still-required explicit y to dispatch. Per-SKU block reason printed in manifest before y/N prompt. 12 unit tests in tests/test_tripo_dispatch_guards.py covering both guards plus escape hatch. renders/output/tripo/QUARANTINE.md written marking all 120 existing outputs as discard-do-not-publish. Cerebrum Do-Not-Repeat entry added 2026-05-11.

error_message: CURS-03: luxury cursor JS loads on immersive templates (slug='immersive') — no exclusion gate in enqueue.php
root_cause: wp_enqueue_script('skyyrose-luxury-cursor', ...) lives inside skyyrose_enqueue_global_scripts() at lines 249-259 with no conditional check. Template slug detection exists (skyyrose_get_current_template_slug() maps template-immersive-*.php to 'immersive') but is not consulted before cursor JS enqueue. Result: cursor JS loads on all pages including immersive templates where the cursor is CSS-hidden.
fix: FIXED in theme 1.1.2 (commit 016f7025f, deployed 2026-05-12). wordpress-theme/skyyrose-flagship/inc/enqueue.php lines 247-263: wrapped the luxury-cursor wp_enqueue_script() block in `if ('immersive' !== skyyrose_get_current_template_slug())`. The template slug helper was already available in the same file (no new dependency). After deploy, live verification confirmed zero <script> tags loading luxury-cursor.js on /experience-black-rose/. Regression test tests/test_luxury_cursor.py::test_cursor_not_loaded_on_immersive flipped from intentional-fail to pass (3/3 green).

error_message: DATA-01: /collection-black-rose/, /collection-love-hurts/, /collection-signature/, /collection-kids-capsule/ all returned HTTP 200 but served the homepage (page-id-9822, page-template-front-page-php) instead of the matching template-collection-*.php. Collection pages were absent from the WordPress DB.
root_cause: skyyrose_run_activation_setup() runs once via add_action('init', ...) at priority 30, gated by `if get_option('skyyrose_activation_setup_version') === SKYYROSE_SETUP_VERSION return;`. The stored option was '4.0.0', matching the constant, so the init runner short-circuited on every request. However, the four collection pages had been deleted from the WP admin at some point (or never created on this site's initial activation), so skyyrose_create_required_pages() never re-ran to recreate them. wp_safe_redirect logic in inc/redirects.php only handled legacy /collections/{slug}/ -> /collection-{slug}/ rewrites — it assumed the canonical destination pages existed, which they did not.
fix: Bumped SKYYROSE_SETUP_VERSION constant from '4.0.0' to '4.1.0' in inc/theme-activation-setup.php (commit 016f7025f, theme 1.1.2). On next request after deploy the init runner sees the version mismatch, fires skyyrose_run_activation_setup(), which calls skyyrose_create_required_pages(). That function iterates the slug map (already includes the four collection slugs at lines 69-88) and runs wp_insert_post() for any slug returned as missing by get_page_by_path(), assigning _wp_page_template meta to point at the matching template-collection-*.php. Post-deploy verified live: /collection-black-rose/ = page-id-9454, love-hurts=9455, signature=9456, kids-capsule=9651 — all routing to their correct templates.
regression_test: scripts/verify_live_structure.py COLLECTION_HERO_ASSETS asserts img[src*='sr-collection-*.webp'] per collection

error_message: Stop hook error: Hook JSON output validation failed — (root): Invalid input
root_cause: Stop hook emitted {hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext: ...} JSON. That schema is valid only for UserPromptSubmit and PostToolUse. Stop accepts only {decision, reason, continue, stopReason, suppressOutput, systemMessage}. Claude Code schema validator rejected the unknown root-level shape.
fix: Replaced jq stdout JSON emission with stderr printf. Stop fires after Claude finishes, so additionalContext injection is moot — the reminder is for the human, stderr is the right channel. Stdout now empty, schema validator passes. Marker file deletion preserved.

error_message: NOT SUPPORTED: option missingRefs. Pass empty schema with $id that should be ignored to ajv.addSchema. TypeError: Cannot set properties of undefined (setting 'defaultMeta') at ajvOrig
root_cause: ESLint 9.39.3 bundles ajv that rejects the 'missingRefs' option used by @eslint/eslintrc's legacy config loader. Lint-staged invokes ESLint 9 which loads eslintrc-universal.cjs which calls ajvOrig with NOT-SUPPORTED options -> hard crash before any file is linted. Blocks pre-commit on any staged .ts/.tsx under frontend/**
fix: Scoped npm overrides in root package.json: 'eslint': {'ajv': '^6.14.0'} AND '@eslint/eslintrc': {'ajv': '^6.14.0'}. Forces both packages to nest ajv v6.15.0 in their own node_modules while keeping top-level ajv@8.18.0 for other consumers. Verified: 'cd frontend && npx eslint <file>' runs to completion (2 warnings, 0 errors, exit 0). Fixed in chore/p1-p5-backlog at TBD-commit.

error_message: error: 'tuple' object is not callable (TestModules in tests/aos/shell/test_commands.py)
root_cause: ModuleRegistry.manifests and .registered_types are @property accessors, not methods; _cmd_modules called them with () which invoked the returned tuple/frozenset.
fix: Dropped the parentheses: kernel.modules.manifests / kernel.modules.registered_types accessed as properties.

error_message: Phase 14 INFRA-01 regression: scripts/nano_banana/catalog.py bypasses skyyrose.core.catalog_loader (independent csv.DictReader) — single-source-of-truth contract broken; Plan 14-02 SUMMARY claim false
root_cause: Plan 14-02 added a catalog_loader shim (commit 51dc3ff8b). Later commits a22074ab3 and 8737e3714 overwrote scripts/nano_banana/catalog.py with a standalone csv.DictReader parser (lines 8, 26-55), regressing INFRA-01. Data stays consistent (same canonical CSV file) but the three pipelines no longer share the catalog adapter — the contract, not the data, is violated.
fix: Option A applied. Rewrote scripts/nano_banana/catalog.py load_catalog() + module docstring + CATALOG_CSV constant to source rows via skyyrose.core.catalog_loader.read_catalog_rows / bool_col / CATALOG_CSV (env-overridable). Removed the local csv.DictReader. Preserved the richer HEAD functions untouched (find_source_image, find_back_source, load_products, load_specs, get_material_spec — load-bearing per cli.py/produce_async.py) and kept engine_override + back_source_override; added garment_type from garment_type_lock (INFRA-04 alignment). Added CI regression gate tests/test_catalog_csv_integrity.py::test_nano_banana_catalog_routes_through_core_loader asserting nb.read_catalog_rows IS core.read_catalog_rows, canonical CATALOG_CSV, and no csv.DictReader in load_catalog. Verified: 21/21 integrity tests green incl. test_python_loaders_agree_on_sku_set; nano+elite agree on 33 SKUs; black/ruff/isort clean.

error_message: 5 prod modules reference data/product-catalog.csv as 'single source of truth' but that file is NOT on the main tree and NOT git-tracked (only the canonical wordpress-theme/skyyrose-flagship/data/skyyrose-catalog.csv is tracked)
root_cause: Legacy second-CSV references predating the catalog_loader consolidation. error_message: (eval):17: PIPESTATUS[0]: parameter not set  — verification harness for devworkflows scripts failed under set -u
file: scripts/devworkflows/ (verification harness, not the scripts themselves)
root_cause: The Bash tool executes commands via zsh (env Shell: zsh), not bash. PIPESTATUS is a bash-only array; zsh exposes pipe exit codes as the lowercase 1-indexed `pipestatus` array. With `set -u`, referencing the unset PIPESTATUS made the harness abort. The 3 devworkflow scripts were unaffected (they run under their own `#!/usr/bin/env bash` shebang) — only the inline test harness tripped.
fix: In Bash-tool one-liners, never use ${PIPESTATUS[*]}. Capture exit status directly: run the command, then `rc=$?` on the next line (no pipe), or redirect to a file and read $? immediately. Reserve PIPESTATUS for code inside `bash script.sh` files.

error_message: AssertionError: assert '' == 'skyyrose-assets' — tests/services/test_r2_client.py::TestR2Config::test_default_bucket (full-suite only)
root_cause: Per-domain .env scaffold emitted unfilled keys as bare 'KEY='. skyyrose.core.env_loader.load_project_env lists skyyrose/.env in its load order; load_dotenv(override=False) still SETS a key not already present, so 'KEY=' injected an explicit empty string into global os.environ. r2_client's os.getenv('R2_BUCKET', DEFAULT_BUCKET) then returned '' instead of 'skyyrose-assets', polluting full-suite test ordering. error_message: Adversarial review of the new deterministic fraud scorer (services/risk/fraud.py) found 16 real defects. Top: (HIGH) never-raises contract broken — `raw = sum(s.weight for s in signals)` sat OUTSIDE the try/except, so FraudScorer(weights={'x':'str'}).assess(...) raised TypeError; (HIGH) false-positive — a legit first-time guest sending a $500+ gift internationally stacked country(20)+name(10)+highvalue(10)+firsttime(15)=55 -> HIGH/review, costing real sales; (MEDIUM) mixed naive/aware date_created silently zeroed ALL velocity signals (TypeError caught -> every history row out-of-window); (MEDIUM) raw email/IP/full names leaked into signal detail strings + as_dict; (MEDIUM) markdown pipe-injection in format_assessment; (LOW) NaN total treated as 0-risk; current order counted in its own velocity; guest==None false-positive; stale hardcoded total_tools.

Added 9 regression tests.

error_message: Adversarial review of the 3 remaining Tier-2 scorers (retention/forecasting/personalization) found 43 confirmed findings (6 HIGH/13 MED/24 LOW; heavy dedup -> ~16 distinct). Top: (HIGH x3, same root) LOYAL lifecycle stage UNREACHABLE past at_risk_days=90 because recency gates fired before the loyalty frequency check -> a 5-order buyer in a normal 91-180d inter-drop gap was demoted to AT_RISK and routed to win-back (100% false-positive on loyal buyers for SkyyRose's quarterly drop cadence); (HIGH) pre-order products triggered false CRITICAL 'reorder_now' alerts because is_preorder was captured but never consulted in sellout risk; (HIGH) personalization popularity used raw unbounded count -> a bestseller could swamp personalized signals; (MED) forecast window included today (partial day) inflating velocity; forecast_units_30d used self.horizon_days not literal 30; _price used `or` dropping price=0.0; segment_customers/forecast_sales fallbacks called now() per row (non-deterministic batch); _parse_dt dropped datetime objects; (HIGH, verified-but-out-of-scope) orphaned analytics_forecast_sales ToolSpec -> documented canonical surface rather than building dispatch.
root_cause: Green TDD proved each rule fires in isolation but never tested cross-band interactions (stage-ordering precedence), domain calibration (drop cadence, pre-order semantics), bounded scales (raw-count swamp), batch determinism, or real-world datetime types. Adversarial review with domain context surfaced them.
fix: Retention: loyalty check moved before the at_risk recency gate so loyal buyers stay LOYAL through the inter-drop window (degrade only past dormant/churned); churn_risk=0 when frequency<=0; _parse_dt accepts datetime. Forecasting: is_preorder -> SelloutRisk.NONE; window excludes today (recency>=1); forecast_units_30d fixed at literal 30d (removed dead horizon_days ctor param); removed unreachable _trend branch; _parse_dt accepts datetime. Personalization: popularity log1p-scaled; _price uses explicit None; added similarity_k fanout param. analytics_agent: forecast_sales/segment_customers take as_of (single now -> reproducible batch), segment_customers rejects non-rfm methods gracefully, ToolSpec metadata documented. Added 15 regression tests (148 total green).

error_message: Commit 6eab086ed (brand-learning ApprovalGate fix) orphaned — not in HEAD ancestry, changes gone from working tree

error_message: Cleanup classifier marked all 81 untracked files as 'on main, differs' (KEEP=0) — would have recommended deleting 76 genuine local files = data loss
root_cause: Used `git rev-parse origin/main:$f` as an existence test. For a path NOT in the tree, git rev-parse ECHOES the unparseable string to stdout (errors on stderr) instead of returning empty, so every file looked present. Real blob hash never matched the echoed string -> universal false 'differs'.
fix: Use `git cat-file -e origin/main:$f` (or `git ls-tree`) for path existence — does not echo. Re-classified: only 4 (deleted) + regen-boost (deleted) were truly on main; 76 are genuine local work, kept.

error_message: FLUX LoRA Replicate API contract bugs would 404/422 on first real call (lr_scheduler, guidance_scale, flux-dev model, version-less URL, missing destination)
root_cause: Payloads/endpoints were written from memory, not the live schema: lr_scheduler & guidance_scale are not valid fields; base flux-dev lacks LoRA fields; trainings require /versions/{id}/trainings and a destination.

error_message: Turbopack build failed: Route segment config "dynamic" is not compatible with nextConfig.cacheComponents. Please remove it.
root_cause: Added `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` to new catalog API routes, but frontend/next.config.ts sets cacheComponents:true, which forbids route-segment config exports (routes are dynamic by default; opt into caching with 'use cache').
fix: Removed the `export const dynamic` exports. For GET /api/catalog (which had no inherent dynamism and built as Static ○, risking a stale snapshot after a CSV write) added `await connection()` from next/server to force request-time execution → route flipped to dynamic ƒ. Context7-confirmed API.

error_message: Build error: Both middleware file "./middleware.ts" and proxy file "./proxy.ts" are detected. Please use "./proxy.ts" only.
root_cause: Created frontend/middleware.ts to add an API auth gate, not knowing Next 16 renamed middleware->proxy. The project's gate already lived in frontend/proxy.ts (renamed during the Next 16 migration). The two files conflict — Next 16 forbids both.
fix: Deleted frontend/middleware.ts; extended the existing frontend/proxy.ts instead — added an /api/* 401-JSON branch and widened the matcher from ['/admin/:path*'] to ['/admin/:path*','/api/((?!auth|checkout).*)']. Excludes /api/auth (NextAuth) and /api/checkout (public) so login + checkout keep working.

error_message: All /api/v1/training/* endpoints returned HTTP 500 (coroutine '_find_training_versions' was never awaited; TypeError 'coroutine' object is not iterable). After await fix: ValueError: Out of range float values are not JSON compliant: inf
root_cause: A prior perf refactor (a16ee1d67) converted the file helpers to async (aiofiles/asyncio.to_thread) but left every call site synchronous -> each endpoint received a coroutine object (truthy), so guards passed and iteration/attr-access raised -> caught -> 500. Bug-behind-a-bug: TrainingProgressResponse.best_loss defaulted to float('inf'), which is not JSON-compliant; the await bug masked it because requests never reached serialization. imagery/training_progress_reporter.py writes real progress.json with best_loss=Infinity, so live data hits this too.
fix: Added 'await' to all 9 helper call sites (_find_training_versions/_load_progress_file/_load_status_file/_load_round_table_results). Made best_loss Optional[float] default None + field_validator coercing non-finite (inf/-inf/nan) -> None. Also wired POST /training/export to real huggingface_hub HfApi.create_repo+upload_file (dataset) via asyncio.to_thread, honest fallback: missing HF_TOKEN -> local-only success with dataset_url=None; HF failure -> 502; real URL only from a real RepoUrl. 13 tests added (tests/api/test_training_status.py). Dashboard get_training_status() consumer was also repaired by this fix.

error_message: V7 product cards in grid row 2+ never auto-advance (carousel stalls)
root_cause: IntersectionObserver threshold:0.2 on ~713px-tall cards — a partially-visible card never crosses 20% so its autoplay timer never (re)starts
fix: threshold:0 — any pixel on screen starts autoplay; verified row-2 cards resume on scrollIntoView

error_message: model-viewer: web GLBs fail — 'setMeshoptDecoder must be called' + basis texture CORS block
root_cause: model-viewer v4 does NOT enable meshopt by default (EXT_meshopt_compression), and its gstatic basis transcoder (KHR_texture_basisu) is CORS-blocked from a localhost origin
fix: set self.ModelViewerElement.meshoptDecoderLocation + ktx2TranscoderLocation BEFORE the module loads; VENDOR meshopt_decoder.js + basis_transcoder.js/.wasm locally (renders/3d/_viewer/) and point at same-origin paths to avoid CORS

error_message: Blender Solidify batch on Meshy GLBs blew up scale (sg-002 bbox 2->2709 units)
root_cause: Meshy GLBs carry tiny root-node scale; use_even_offset amplified it; transforms not applied before modifier
fix: ABANDONED - solidify is the wrong lever (slab != garment). Flat single-photo Meshy models cannot gain real depth without re-render; serve verified 2D hub photo instead. If cleanup-batch needed, run on genuinely-3D SKUs only (flatness>0.35): transform_apply first, normals+smooth+recenter, NO solidify.

error_message: V7 card: Kids Capsule accent rendered Signature gold (#D4AF37); v7 card-type filter registered unconditionally while assets slug-gated (broken cards on WC-native surfaces)

fix: KC accent -> #B76E79 rose-gold (brand canon) + caps Bebas; added skyyrose_v7_is_active_surface() shared by both the enqueue AND the card-type filter; also label dedup via skyyrose_get_collections_config, PHP8 key guards, carousel aria-hidden, filter-value allowlist at call-sites

error_message: model-viewer GLB load times out in browser; only 8/33 models render (glb-models.html)
root_cause: Two compounding causes: (1) meshopt-compressed GLBs need the meshopt decoder, which model-viewer only loads when meshoptDecoderLocation is configured BEFORE the first <model-viewer> element is created; (2) opening the page via file:// blocks fetch() of the GLBs and WASM workers (Chrome CORS on file:// origin), so nothing loads regardless of decoder config.
fix: Configure self.ModelViewerElement.meshoptDecoderLocation/ktx2TranscoderLocation/dracoDecoderLocation to vendored local paths (renders/3d/_viewer/) in a <script> placed before the model-viewer module <script> (lines 4-9), and serve the page over HTTP from the repo root (python3 -m http.server) instead of file://. Verified via Playwright: 33/33 model-viewers loaded, 0 load errors after scroll; geometry renders correctly (br-001 crewneck, br-002 joggers, br-003 jersey, etc.). The 8/33 initial count is loading="lazy" working as designed (IntersectionObserver), not a failure.

error_message: ImportError: cannot import name 'WordPressWooCommerceClient' from 'integrations.wordpress_client' — CommerceAgent WooCommerce integration is dead on main (WORDPRESS_AVAILABLE=False), so commerce/products/bulk returns per-product 'client not connected' at runtime even though the wire is correct.
root_cause: Pre-existing agent<->client interface mismatch. agents/commerce_agent.py:48 does `from integrations.wordpress_client import WordPressWooCommerceClient` (try/except ImportError -> WORDPRESS_AVAILABLE=False fallback at line 53), but integrations/wordpress_client.py defines the class as WordPressClient (no WordPressWooCommerceClient alias). Verified via `python -c import`: ImportError + WORDPRESS_AVAILABLE=False + client=None. Additionally CommerceAgent._ensure_wordpress_client calls `await client.connect()`, but WordPressClient has no connect() — it exposes an async context-manager API (__aenter__/__aexit__, close()). So even fixing the name, the connection lifecycle is wrong. Discovered while wiring T3-2 commerce/products/bulk; the 3 wire tests mock the agent so they pass while the real integration is non-functional. api/dashboard.py:811-917 imports the same broken name.
fix: FIXED 2026-06-28 (founder approved full fix). (1) integrations/wordpress_client.py: added module-level alias `WordPressWooCommerceClient = WordPressClient` + a `WordPressClient.from_env()` classmethod (reads WORDPRESS_URL/WP_SITE_URL + WC_CONSUMER_KEY/WOOCOMMERCE_KEY + WC_CONSUMER_SECRET/WOOCOMMERCE_SECRET; raises ValueError when missing). (2) agents/commerce_agent.py _ensure_wordpress_client: dropped the non-existent `.connect()`, now constructs via `WordPressWooCommerceClient.from_env()` (honors an injected client, degrades gracefully on missing creds keeping _wordpress_connected=False). (3) api/dashboard.py: 3 sites swapped `WordPressWooCommerceClient()` -> `.from_env()`. Verified: import resolves (is WordPressClient True), WORDPRESS_AVAILABLE flipped False->True, from_env graceful-raise on missing creds; black+ruff clean on all 3 files; rtk proxy pytest tests/api/test_commerce_bulk_wire.py + tests/test_core_agents.py = 231 passed (no regression). optimize_price (in main) uses ml_module not _ensure_wordpress_client, so unaffected.

error_message: Stop-hook RED locally: tests/mcp/test_wc_client.py::test_missing_env_raises_keyerror DID NOT RAISE KeyError. Local-only — CI was green.
root_cause: Follow-on to bug-165's WC unification (commit 7426334dc). WCCredentials.from_env() called a helper _ensure_wordpress_env_loaded() that load_dotenv'd .env.wordpress BEFORE reading os.environ. On a dev machine .env.wordpress exists, so the auto-load repopulated the exact credential vars the test had just cleared via monkeypatch.delenv -> from_env resolved creds -> KeyError never raised -> test failed. In CI .env.wordpress is gitignored/absent, so the load no-op'd and the test passed: a test whose outcome depends on an untracked file's presence is non-deterministic. A parallel session compounded confusion by editing the same files (kept auto-load + stubbed the helper in a new test) while this session removed the auto-load.
fix: FIXED 2026-06-29, committed 7bfce75cf direct to main (founder: 'commit the wc fix to main'). (1) wc_safe_client.py: removed _ensure_wordpress_env_loaded entirely; from_env() now reads os.environ ONLY (pure), so callers/tests fully control the env via monkeypatch. (2) devskyy_mcp.py: the application bootstrap now does load_dotenv(Path(__file__).parent/'.env.wordpress') after imports (with noqa: E402 on the post-load mcp_tools imports) — the file load belongs to the app, not the dataclass. (3) tests/mcp/test_wc_client.py: test_missing_env_raises_keyerror + smoketest missing-env test clear all SEVEN var-names from_env accepts (WC_BASE_URL/WORDPRESS_URL/WP_SITE_URL + WC_/WOOCOMMERCE_ key+secret). (4) tests/test_wc_credentials_from_env.py: dropped the now-dead setattr stub of the removed helper; the delenv loop is the entire isolation. Verified: PYTEST_EXIT=0 (25/25 across both WC test files), ruff clean on all 4 files; pushed da73ed5ab..7bfce75cf fast-forward; re-confirmed on origin/main (helper=0, stub=0, all-7-cleared, bootstrap load present).

root_cause: Snapshot-pinned tests rotted vs evolving SoT. asset_hub froze the 2026-06-25 manifest counts + hardcoded SKU verdicts; manifest since promoted to 73 verified/20 pending. wc_client from_env() auto-loaded symlinked .env.wordpress, defeating monkeypatch.delenv.

error_message: Live skyyrose.co homepage: 404 on black-rose-sherpa-jacket-front-model.webp (br-006 Black Rose jacket). 1 of 33 homepage images broken; fires twice in console.
root_cause: Phantom subdirectory in the image path. front-page.php (lines 308, 485, 521) and data/render-keepers.json:12 referenced .../images/products/black-rose-sherpa-jacket/black-rose-sherpa-jacket-front-model.webp — with an extra black-rose-sherpa-jacket/ folder that does not exist in the theme asset tree (all product images are flat in products/). Vision-verified the flat-path file is the correct garment (black Black-Rose hooded jacket with grayscale rose/wings chest crest), so the bug is the path, not the asset.
fix: Collapsed the phantom subdir to the flat path in both files: front-page.php (3 occurrences via replace_all) and render-keepers.json (br-006 asset line) now point at /images/products/black-rose-sherpa-jacket-front-model.webp (the HTTP 200 file). Verified: 0 remaining subdir refs in wordpress-theme/, render-keepers.json re-parses as valid JSON, no other products/<slug>/<slug> double-subdir image paths in front-page.php. Source-only fix (PHP/JSON, no .min rebuild). Live still 404s until theme deploy (STOP-AND-SHOW gated).

error_message: Tying hub to SOT: hub override silently fell back — sot.json kept the catalog image for the 7 synced renders. served_theme_path returned the source suffix (.png), but sync_hub_to_theme transcodes to .webp, so resolve_asset() found no file and the override was skipped.

fix: served_theme_path always returns assets/images/products/hub/<sku>-<face>.webp for off-theme verified renders; added regression test test_served_theme_path_offtheme_render_is_always_webp.

error_message: br-011 (Jersey Series 4 Hockey) has garment_type_lock='hoodie' but is a jersey per registry+_JERSEY_SKUS
root_cause: Jersey membership in 3 unsynced places (CSV garment_type_lock, registry sku_folders, _JERSEY_SKUS). br-011 CSV=hoodie, other two=jersey. Same redundant-field disease as br-014/15.

error_message: Glyphs built from potrace-vectorized PNG cells rendered upside-down (PIL ImageFont render showed an inverted A) despite a sane-looking bbox
root_cause: potrace's SVG <g transform="translate(...) scale(...)"> repositions/scales the path on the page but does not convert SVG's y-down convention into font y-up/baseline-up space -- bbox math looked correct in both orientations so a coordinate sanity check could not have caught it; only rendering the actual glyph and reading the pixels surfaced the flip.
fix: Added a second, explicit Transform().scale(1, -1) after potrace's own translate/scale, plus Cu2QuPen(reverse_direction=True) for correct hole winding on O/A/B/g. Verified by rendering 8 test glyphs (straight lines, curves, holes, descender) with PIL.ImageFont and reading the PNG pixels.

error_message: build_font() crashed with a bare AssertionError deep in fontTools, or silently mis-mapped glyphs, on malformed/ambiguous manifests
root_cause: No validation of manifest cell uniqueness (duplicate char/codepoint -> glyphOrder/glyf length mismatch -> fontTools AssertionError), no bbox bounds checking (OOB bbox silently read as black-padded ink via Image.crop), and Image.open() had no decode-error wrapping (PIL.UnidentifiedImageError leaked past the CLI's narrow except tuple as a raw traceback). Found by an adversarial code-review workflow agent and reproduced.
fix: Added _validate_manifest() (dup char/codepoint, missing keys, OOB bbox -> all raise clear ValueError) called before any processing; wrapped Image.open() to normalize decode failures to ValueError. Verified all 4 new fail-loud paths raise ValueError (not a crash, not silent corruption) with a standalone test script.

error_message: OpenAI gpt-image-2 images.edit() call returns 400 'The model gpt-image-2 does not support the input_fidelity parameter' (code invalid_input_fidelity_model)
root_cause: OAIImageClient.edit() (client.py:74-85) unconditionally sends input_fidelity=config.INPUT_FIDELITY ('high', config.py:37) on every edit() call. config.py's comment claims this is 'verified via Context7... supported for gpt-image-1.5+ (incl. gpt-image-2)' but the LIVE API rejects it for gpt-image-2 specifically -- reproduced 24/24 times in a real call. Context7 (openai/openai-openapi, images/edits schema) lists input_fidelity support for gpt-image-1 / gpt-image-1-mini / gpt-image-1.5 only -- gpt-image-2 is not in that list. This means every product-render edit() call through this client is currently failing the same way, separate from any font-generation work.
fix: NOT YET FIXED (discovered incidentally during an unrelated font-pilot task, flagged to founder rather than patched unprompted since it touches the shared production render pipeline). Workaround used in the font pilot: bypass OAIImageClient.edit() and call openai.OpenAI().images.edit() directly, omitting input_fidelity entirely. Real fix needed in client.py/config.py: stop sending input_fidelity when model=='gpt-image-2', or make it conditional on model name.

error_message: .venv/bin/python: No such file or directory (Stop hook), then No module named pytest, then boto3/langgraph/scikit-image import errors during collection
file: .venv/ (deleted, untracked — same territory as the 135-file working-tree deletion anomaly flagged earlier this session), pyproject.toml
root_cause: .venv was fully deleted from disk (untracked, not a git operation). pip install -e ".[all]" resolves an enormous dep graph (torch/langchain/mineru/bentoml/xxhash-fallback-chain) that stalled for 6+min on source builds before I killed it; boto3 (used by integrations/cloudflare_r2_manager.py) was never declared in any pyproject.toml extra despite being required by the mcp extra import chain.
fix: Recreated .venv, installed base package + boto3 + langgraph + scikit-image directly (piecemeal, faster than full [all] resolve) instead of waiting out the slow full extras install. Declared boto3>=1.43.0 in the mcp extra of pyproject.toml so future installs pick it up without manual patching.
